docs.title: Chad Eline
docs.text:
Senior Data Engineer | Risk Technology & Analytics | Python, Spark & Cloud

I'm a data engineer who thrives at the intersection of finance and technology. Over 12+ years, I've migrated legacy SAS systems to modern Python architectures, built pipelines that process hundreds of millions of records daily, and delivered regulatory platforms that have resolved Federal Reserve findings. I care deeply about building systems that are not just functional, but maintainable and understood by the teams who inherit them.

docs.title: Core Stack
docs.text:
Languages Python · PySpark · SQL

Cloud & Data Azure · PostgreSQL · Airflow

Tools Docker · Git · Linux

docs.title: What I Do
docs.text:

    Production ETL pipelines, data warehousing, and cloud platform migrations. This is my core craft—building systems that process hundreds of millions of records reliably.

  • Risk Management

    GARP FRM certified; CCAR, Basel, and IFRS9 implementations

  • Forecasting & Analytics

    Balance sheet, income statement, and capital planning models

  • Applied ML

    End-to-end ML pipelines using OCR, NLP, and XGBoost in production

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Senior Data Engineer with 10+ years modernizing financial and regulatory analytics platforms in highly regulated environments. Proven track record leading migrations from SAS and on-premise systems to Python and Azure-based architectures. Deep experience translating risk and finance requirements into scalable data pipelines, forecasting models, and production-grade analytical systems.

docs.title: Risk Consultant II
docs.text:
The Financial Risk Group · Cary, NC · Dec 2020 – Jan 2026

This role split into two distinct phases. The first three years focused on migrating a client's regulatory reporting application to Azure—a SAS-based system with a custom scheduler and web UI. The second phase involved SAS-to-Python conversions on FRG's internal platform across multiple banking clients. Every migration concluded with parallel stress cycles, reconciling dataset level differences. These projects reinforced that technical execution is only half the job—the other half is change management, building trust with modeling teams through rigorous validation and clear communication.

  • Owned migration of analytical and regulatory models from SAS/legacy systems into Python-based cloud architecture, ensuring model parity, validation, and stakeholder sign-off
  • Built production data pipeline using OCR, NLP, and XGBoost to convert unstructured regulatory documents into structured, queryable datasets
  • Designed and implemented Python-based PySpark job orchestration framework to improve batch reliability, monitoring, and scalability across forecasting workflows
  • Led development of Balance Sheet, Income Statement, and PPNR forecasting platforms for regulatory capital planning and reporting

Company MVP 2023

Led large-scale migration from legacy on-premise infrastructure to Microsoft Azure:

  • Basel, CCAR, and IFRS models
  • Model backtesting and monitoring frameworks
  • ETL pipelines and enterprise data warehouse
  • De-identification and PII handling for security compliance

docs.title: Risk Consultant I
docs.text:
The Financial Risk Group · Cary, NC · Jun 2016 – Dec 2020

This period centered on a multi-year consolidation effort—unifying fragmented risk platforms into a single governed system. The starting point was typical of legacy environments: manually versioned codebases, Excel files, scattered databases, and upstream systems without SLAs. One project involved untangling a complex Excel workbook into a maintainable codebase on their internal platform. I developed the project plan, executed the translation, shepherded it through QA and validation, and supported the production release. The 4-hour-to-15-minute pipeline optimization came from partitioning and indexing, and reducing I/O by pushing processing back into the database where possible.

  • Delivered CCAR, DFAST, and IFRS9 analytical systems supporting capital planning and regulatory compliance for large financial institutions
  • Consolidated forecasting platforms, retiring fragmented legacy systems and centralizing reporting pipelines into maintainable architectures
  • Optimized high-volume database ingestion pipeline, reducing runtime from 4 hours to 15 minutes through partitioning, indexing, and parallelization
  • Partnered with finance and risk stakeholders to define business requirements, translate regulatory logic, and validate model assumptions before production deployment

docs.title: Data Warehouse Solutions Architect
docs.text:
Bank of America · Charlotte, NC · Jan 2015 – Jun 2016

The $17B LCR impact came from giving the bank accurate visibility into their FDIC requirements—prior to this project, they were significantly over-estimating. The technical challenge was data volume and lag logic: a single day's calculation required multiple days of account-level deposit history. The bottleneck was I/O, so I pushed processing back into the database and only pulled aggregated results when necessary.

  • Designed FDIC calculation engine processing 163 million records daily, reducing LCR outflow by $17B and resolving a Federal Reserve MRA
  • Led ETL and data warehouse architecture for 40+ onshore and offshore developers; standardized development practices
  • Devised technical solution for Bank of America's Federal Reserve 5G Liquidity report
  • Redesigned Operational Deposits data warehouse feed, reducing runtime from 4 hours to 55 minutes

Platinum Global Recognition Award

Leadership and execution in resolving the FDIC MRA

Recognition

Risk Consultant I - The Financial Risk Group, Cary, NC (Jun 2016 – Dec 2020) - Delivered CCAR, DFAST, and IFRS9 analytics solutions supporting regulatory compliance and capital planning - Designed forecasting platforms consolidating and retiring multiple legacy systems for Income Statement and Balance Sheet reporting - Optimized high-volume database loads, reducing one critical pipeline from 4 hours to 15 minutes via partitioning and parallel processing

Data Warehouse Solutions Architect - Bank of America, Charlotte, NC (Jan 2015 – Jun 2016) - Designed FDIC calculation engine processing 163M records daily, reducing LCR outflow by $17B and resolving Federal Reserve MRA - Awarded Platinum Global Recognition Award for leadership in resolving FDIC MRA - Led ETL and data warehouse architecture for 40+ developers; standardized development practices across organization - Redesigned Operational Deposits data warehouse feed, reducing runtime from 4 hours to 55 minutes

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: A showcase of personal projects and open-source tools.

docs.title: Data Engineering
docs.text:
• Public Financial Data Pipeline

    End-to-end ETL pipeline using public financial data sources (FRED, SEC EDGAR) with medallion architecture, Airflow orchestration, and data quality validation.

    • Medallion Architecture: Bronze → Silver → Gold layers
    • Public APIs: FRED economic indicators + SEC EDGAR filings
    • Data Quality: Great Expectations validation at each layer
    • Containerized: Docker Compose for reproducible deployment

    Stack: Python, Polars, DuckDB, Airflow, Docker

    View on GitHub

docs.text:
• Resume Builder

    Generate polished MS Word resumes from Markdown files with customizable YAML templates. Separates content from styling—write once, output to multiple formats.

    • Write content in Markdown, style separately
    • Multiple output formats (DOCX, PDF)
    • Template inheritance and overrides
    • Cover letter support

    Stack: Python, python-docx
